name: Pain Au Citron (Lemon Bread)
recipeIngredient: 1 Lemon zest; grated zest from 1 lemon
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Rustic European bread From Your Bread Machine Book by Linda West Eckhardt and Diana Collingwood Butts
Dough Setting Makes two 6 x 4 inches oval loaves
The addition of lemon zest to a rye and wheat bread yields a citrus-scented bread you'll love with seafood and pates. Shape the bread like big lemons. The crust will be golden brown, and the crumb dense and aromatic. The bread keeps at least 3 days wrapped in nothing but a paper bag.
1. Place all ingredients in the bread machine pan and process on the dough setting. Remove to a lightly floured surface and punch down. Knead a moment or two.
2. Divide the dough into two pieces. Shape each piece into an oval shape with pointed ends - like lemons. Place on parchment paper sprinkled with cornmeal and set on a baking sheet. Cover with plastic wrap and let the dough rise in a warm, draft-free place until almost doubled in bulk, no more than 1 hour.
3. Make three quick slashes in the top of each risen loaf.
4. Preheat the oven to 450°F. Bake until golden brown and done through, about 30 minutes. Remove to a rack and cool thoroughly before slicing.
